summary: Matched a batch of small Yoshi special-move functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c, replacing several placeholder asm markers with simple physics, collision, and animation wrappers. The decomp.dev report credited 13 new GALE01 matches for +572 bytes and +0.01% matched code. Although the PR title emphasizes ftYs_SpecialN, the diff also matched Yoshi SpecialHi_Anim, SpecialLw_Anim, and SpecialLwLanding_Anim.
what_changed: Implemented several previously placeholder physics callbacks in ftYs_SpecialN.c: ftYs_SpecialAirN1_0_Phys, ftYs_SpecialN1_1_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Phys, ftYs_SpecialN2_1_Phys, and ftYs_SpecialAirN2_1_Phys.
Grounded Yoshi neutral-special physics wrappers call ft_80084F3C after casting the ftYs_GObj to Fighter_GObj; aerial neutral-special physics wrappers call ft_80084EEC with the same cast pattern.
Implemented collision wrappers ftYs_SpecialN1_Coll, ftYs_SpecialN1_1_Coll, ftYs_SpecialN2_1_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirN2_1_Coll, and ftYs_SpecialAirN2_0_Coll.

Ground collision wrappers use ft_8008403C with callback functions such as fn_8012D128, fn_8012D230, and fn_8012D3C0; air collision wrappers use ft_80082C74 with callbacks such as fn_8012D488 and fn_8012D4F0.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialHi_Anim with GET_FIGHTER, a held-input check against 0x200 that increments fp->mv.ys.specialn.x4, and an end-of-animation transition through ft_8008A2BC.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialLw_Anim as a frame-remaining check that calls ftYs_SpecialS_8012EA04 when the animation ends.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Anim as a frame-remaining check that returns to the standard transition via ft_8008A2BC when the landing animation ends.
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h callback prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void callback signatures for fn_8012D128, fn_8012D230, fn_8012D3C0, and fn_8012D488.
smart_moves: Harvested many exact matches from tiny wrapper functions rather than trying to solve larger unknown bodies first.
Used the existing ground-versus-air helper split consistently: ft_80084F3C for grounded physics and ft_80084EEC for aerial physics.
Used the existing ground-versus-air collision helper split consistently: ft_8008403C for ground collision state changes and ft_80082C74 for air collision state changes.
Typed callback prototypes in the header once the call sites showed the concrete gobj argument type, reducing reliance on UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS.
Preserved stack-shape/matching needs in animation functions with explicit unused padding arrays, as seen in ftYs_SpecialHi_Anim and ftYs_SpecialLw_Anim.
Reused common fighter animation-end idioms: ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(...) == 0 followed by either ft_8008A2BC or a Yoshi-specific transition helper.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special-move files, many remaining asm placeholders may be single-call wrappers around shared fighter physics/collision helpers; matching them can be done by comparing adjacent state variants.
Ground and air variants often differ only by which shared helper is called, while retaining the same GObj cast style.

Collision callbacks can reveal function signatures: passing fn_8012D128-style symbols into collision helpers supported converting several header declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void callback prototypes.
Animation callbacks commonly match with a minimal pattern: optional state/input update, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining check, then transition helper.
Do not over-name address-based local callbacks without stronger evidence; this PR kept fn_8012D128-style names while only improving their types.
PR titles may underspecify the touched states; inspect the diff because this SpecialN file also contained SpecialHi and SpecialLw animation matches.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter symbols use the ftYs_ prefix.
Grounded special variants use names like ftYs_SpecialN*, while aerial variants insert Air, e.g. ftYs_SpecialAirN*.
Neutral-special substates are encoded with N1/N2 and suffixes such as _0, _1, and _2, e.g. ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Phys.
Callback functions without semantic names remain address-style fn_8012D128 symbols, but can still receive concrete prototypes.
Function suffixes identify callback role: _Phys for physics, _Coll for collision, _Anim for animation, and _Enter for state entry.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace placeholder comments of the form /// #symbol with minimal C bodies when the function is a one-call wrapper.
Use explicit casts from ftYs_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* to match helper signatures and generated code.
Use small unused stack arrays such as u8 _[8]; where needed to preserve stack layout for matching.
Match animation-end logic with ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining((Fighter_GObj*) gobj) == 0 rather than more elaborate control flow.
When a helper takes a callback, update callback prototypes in the header to concrete gobj signatures to make the C call expression type-correct and matchable.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported 13 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN for +572 bytes, raising matched code to 33.57% (+0.01%).
The bot listed all 13 changed functions as improving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining /// # placeholders in src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN.c, especially adjacent Yoshi special callbacks.
Check whether ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Enter, ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Anim, and fn_8012E44C still remain unmatched after this PR.
Search other fighter special files for one-call Phys wrappers using ft_80084F3C and ft_80084EEC.
Search other fighter collision callbacks for ft_8008403C and ft_80082C74 call patterns that can type address-style fn_* callbacks.
Audit remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in ftYs_SpecialN.h for callback signatures inferable from helper calls.
